text:
REGIME — Small caps and commodities led; defensives and housing faded.

INDICES — VIX 25.8, elevated and rising. IWM +0.22%, SPY +0.04%, QQQ +0.02%, DIA -0.19%; small caps beat large caps cleanly. GLD surged +0.97% on the session while VIXY (VIX futures) jumped +3.52%, signaling residual hedging demand beneath the calm surface.

SECTORS — XLE +0.80%, XLK +0.48%, XLU +0.22%, XLV +0.20%, XLC +0.05%, XLF +0.00%, XLRE -0.10%, XLI -0.20%, XLB -0.28%, XLY -1.16%, XLP -1.69%. Top industries: WGMI +2.37%, XES +2.08%; bottom industries: XHB -2.23%, MSOS -8.56%.

THE 7s — Buildout +1.55%, Semi +1.43%, Old Metals +1.14%, Fab +0.79%, Energy +0.71%, Health +0.53%, Power +0.36%, Cloud +0.33%, Bank +0.23%, Industrial +0.07%, New Staples -0.06%, Insurance -0.10%, Freight -0.18%, Software -0.25%, Medtech -0.25%, Toll Road -0.35%, Defense -1.02%, New Metals -1.03%, Old Staples -1.90%, Housing -1.91%. Strongest ticker in Buildout 7 pacing the group; weakest in Housing 7 consistent with XHB's -2.23% session.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.2, Neutral. Top movers: UNH +9.37% (Health, vol spike 2.5x), WGMI +2.37% (Industries), XES +2.08% (Industries). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +7.52%; worst: XLP -4.95%. Best basket 20d: Energy +9.02%; worst: Software -9.85%. UNH's +9.37% spike on 2.5x volume likely driven by managed-care catalyst or legal/earnings development.

WATCH — UNH +9.37%: 2.5x vol spike warrants confirmation; gap sustainability or reversal risk tomorrow. RSPS -1.58% on 3.0x volume: extreme vol divergence from broader small-cap strength flags distribution risk. XLP -1.69%: worst sector, 20d momentum -4.95%, breaking defensive bid despite neutral macro.
